name: Arsenio Rodríguez
abstract: Arsenio Rodríguez (born Ignacio Arsenio Travieso Scull; August 31, 1911 – December 30, 1970) was a Cuban musician, composer and bandleader. He played the tres, as well as the tumbadora, and he specialized in son, rumba and other Afro-Cuban music styles.

concerts.label: Concert of Cuban and Puerto Rican Music

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: That’s my second mentor, which really becomes my first... Arsenio Rodríguez, who is credited for putting the congo next to a bongo, and the new start of conjunto... When you talk about our music, you talk before or after Arsenio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: We were so far advanced... They were too far advanced for Arsenio Rodriguez' ear... Harmonically he was not rich, so we was more advanced, musically, than Arsenio really was.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
